danger_signals.visual_form: Dr. Robotnik's robot minions appear at end of Act 1 in each level
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat; player must defeat obstacle to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Dr. Robotnik or EggRobo confrontation at end of Act 2
danger_signals.meaning: Major danger checkpoint; narrative antagonist confrontation

danger_signals.visual_form: Player loses rings upon taking damage (implicit damage state)
danger_signals.meaning: Health/vulnerability indicator; player is in danger

danger_signals.signal: Fence of light in bonus stages
danger_signals.visual_form: Approaching barrier from bottom of screen
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat of removal from bonus area; time pressure

danger_signals.visual_form: Colored spheres in 3D environment to be avoided
danger_signals.meaning: Hazard; contact results in stage failure or penalty

safety_signals.meaning: Health buffer; player can absorb one hit without losing a life if carrying rings

safety_signals.visual_form: Checkpoint markers that trigger bonus stage access
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression milestone; opportunity for reward access

safety_signals.visual_form: Colored spheres in 3D environment to be touched
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction; touching converts sphere and contributes to stage completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct colored spheres for momentum boost
safety_signals.meaning: Safe movement aid; propels player for navigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Slot machine rewards in second bonus stage type
safety_signals.meaning: Safety enhancement; extra lives and ability enhancements

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sonic-specific level variations and cutscenes differ from Knuckles campaign

faction_indicators.faction: Knuckles the Echidna (protagonist in this game)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Knuckles character model with distinctive echidna design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Knuckles faces EggRobo instead of Robotnik; different narrative cutscenes; fights Sonic in campaign

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple robot minion designs; EggRobo variant (distinct from Robotnik himself)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Robot enemies throughout levels; Death Egg orbital weapon (narrative element)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Robot variant of Dr. Robotnik design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct from standard Robotnik encounters; primary boss in Knuckles campaign
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears as final boss encounter in Knuckles's quest narrative

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Angel Island under threat from Death Egg relaunch
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environment reflects mechanical/industrial intrusion; robot enemies populate levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Robotnik's forces actively occupying and defending zones; bosses positioned to block progression

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Sonic and Knuckles in conflict (narrative opposition)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character selection at title screen divides story; cutscenes differ by protagonist choice
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Knuckles faces different enemies (EggRobo) than Sonic (Robotnik); level progression reflects character-specific antagonisms

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Giant rings in secret passageways; 3D Special Stages with distinct visual presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player directed to explore hidden areas; Special Stages require active color-sphere interaction

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Power transformation achieved (7 Chaos Emeralds collected)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character transforms into Super Sonic or Super Knuckles; visual power-up indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced abilities unlocked; character displays transformed state during gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Altered level layouts between standalone and combined versions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Super Emeralds become accessible; Hyper transformation possible; save feature available; cross-game character control enabled

environmental_storytelling.example: Sonic can jump higher and access shield abilities; Knuckles can glide and climb walls—level architecture accommodates and emphasizes character strengths
Levels include cutscenes that differ based on selected character; Sonic and Knuckles are opponents, so progression narrative diverges
Sonic faces Dr. Robotnik; Knuckles faces EggRobo—different antagonists reinforce faction conflict and character opposition
Mushroom Hill (first zone) and subsequent zones reflect industrial/mechanical intrusion (Death Egg relaunch threat narrative)
Secret areas contain giant rings leading to Special Stages; exploration rewards player with Chaos Emerald opportunities

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Traverses levels at high speed; aims to stop Dr. Robotnik from re-launching Death Egg; encounters Knuckles as narrative obstacle
Traverses levels using glide and climb mechanics; scuffles with EggRobo; opposes Sonic; defends Angel Island
Appears as boss at end of Sonic's level 2 acts; commands robot minions; represents existential threat via Death Egg
Minion of Robotnik; appears as primary boss in Knuckles campaign; distinct visual/mechanical identity from Robotnik

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Level hazards and Act 1 miniboss encounters
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrol and attack; serve as mechanical obstacles; varied designs signal different threat types
Accessed via giant rings; obtaining all seven enables Super transformation; acts as progression mechanic

notes: Sonic & Knuckles uses character mechanic divergence (jump/shield vs. glide/climb) as core world-state manifestation—the same spatial level communicates danger and opportunity differently depending on playable character, reinforcing the 'opposed protagonist' narrative. Visual faction identity is minimal (color, silhouette) but behavioral opposition is pronounced through alternate boss encounters and cutscene narratives. The lock-on technology mechanic creates a meta-layer of world-state: combining cartridges unlocks new level layouts, characters, and transformation tiers, visually signaling expanded narrative scope. Bonus stages use distinct visual color coding (blue=safe, red=danger, yellow=boost) for instant comprehension without tutorial.
